content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_005_page_001.jpg

1. Do not rebuke an older man, but appeal [to him] as a father, [to] younger [men] as brothers,

2. older women as mothers, younger [women] as sisters, in all purity.

3. Honor widows, the [ones] truly [being] widows.

4. But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show godliness to their own house and to give back recompense to their forebears, for this is acceptable before God.

5. Now the [one] truly [being] a widow and having been left alone has hoped in God and continues in the petitions and the prayers night and day;

6. but the [one] living for pleasure has died while living.

7. And command these things, that they may be above reproach.

8. But if anyone does not provide for his own, and especially [for those] of [his] household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.

9. Let a widow be enrolled [who is] not less than sixty years old, [the] wife of one husband,

10. being testified [to] in good works: if she raised children, if she showed hospitality, if she washed [the] feet of [the] holy [ones], if she helped [the] afflicted, if she followed after every good work.

11. But refuse younger widows, for when they are drawn by desire against the Christos, they wish to marry,

12. having judgment because they set aside the first faith.

13. And at the same time also they learn [to be] idle, going about the houses; and not only idle but also gossips and busybodies, speaking the things not proper.

14. I desire therefore [the] younger [widows] to marry, to bear children, to manage [a] house, to give no occasion to the adversary for reproach.

15. For already some have turned aside after the Adversary.

16. If any faithful woman has widows, let her help them, and let not the assembly be burdened, that it may help the [ones] truly [being] widows.

17. Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially the [ones] laboring in word and teaching.

18. For the Scripture says, "You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out [grain]," and, "The laborer [is] worthy of his wages."

19. Do not receive an accusation against an elder except upon [the testimony of] two or three witnesses.

20. The [ones] sinning, rebuke before all, that the rest also may have fear.

21. I solemnly charge [you] before God and Christos Yeshua and the chosen angels, that you keep these things without prejudice, doing nothing according to partiality.

22. Lay hands hastily on no one, and do not share in [the] sins of others. Keep yourself pure.

23. No longer drink water [only], but use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent ailments.

24. The sins of some men are evident, going before [them] to judgment, but [the sins of] some also follow after.

25. Likewise also the good works are evident, and those being otherwise are not able to be hidden.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.9 — "enrolled":** katalegesthō — a technical term for official registration on a list. The "widow" here is not merely a bereaved woman but one enrolled in an order of church-supported widows with specific duties.

2. **v.17 — "double honor":** diplēs timēs — "honor" (timē) can mean both "respect" and "honorarium/payment." The context (v.18 — wages) suggests financial support is included.

3. **v.18 — Two quotations:** The first is Deuteronomy 25:4 (ox muzzling). The second ("The laborer is worthy of his wages") is attributed to "Scripture" but matches Luke 10:7 — suggesting either that Luke's Gospel was already considered Scripture, or that both draw on a common Jesus tradition.

4. **v.22 — "Lay hands hastily on no one":** Refers to ordination — do not ordain someone prematurely. The laying on of hands was the act of commissioning to ministry.

— EMET, AI Translator
